title: Whats up with my copy of End of Evangelion
display_title: Whats up with my copy of End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: What's up with my copy of End of Evangelion?             Quote Post by DanYeomans » Mon Dec 12, 2011 10:30 pm So, this is something that's been bothering me. I obtained a manga entertainment DVD release of End of Evangelion. I popped it in and it's in LETTERBOX. I was outraged. I talked to a guy who has the same copy. He told me I could flip the DVD over and there's the original version on the other side. However I soon found out that my copy does not have this second side. In the special features section on the back of the case it says "letterbox", but im sure that that's not the only option. Did I get ripped off? Is it a pirate copy? Could anyone with a copy of End of Evangelion clarify this for me? Please, I want a high-quality version :'( All help is greatly appreciated!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 12, 2011 11:01 pm The domestic Manga Entertainment release is like that. There is no official anamorphic English release. The second side of Death and Rebirth has a special encyclopedia feature on the second side called Mokuji Interactive. That's what the guy you talked to was referring to. The End of Evangelion is currently out of print and unlicensed in North America. It kind of sucks. A lot of us are hoping someone will pick it up and release a proper DVD/Blu-ray of it.

[]:             Quote Post by DanYeomans » Mon Dec 12, 2011 11:07 pm This is a bit confusing to me. mostly because I've seen high-resolution, anamorphic widescreen versions of it on the web (albeit not on DVD). Where do these people get their video sources from though? Basically though, the version I have is the highest quality I can get ?

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Dec 13, 2011 12:55 am The high quality ones you are seeing online are ripped from the Japanese DVD, which for some reason has no Western equivalent.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 13, 2011 8:08 am DanYeomans wrote: Basically though, the version I have is the highest quality I can get?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Dec 13, 2011 9:27 am rhfxz1s wrote: The high quality ones you are seeing online are ripped from the Japanese DVD, which for some reason has no Western equivalent.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Dec 15, 2011 2:06 am Well, for some reasons the old Italian/German release made by Panini was widescreen though it used the same masters of the Manga U.S. release (it was sub-licensed from Manga after all), maybe they simply cropped the blackbars from the masters, who knows... rhfxz1s wrote: The high quality ones you are seeing online are ripped from the Japanese DVD, which for some reason has no Western equivalent.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Dec 15, 2011 9:20 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: It has no American equivalent, the remastered edition has been released in Italy (which hopefully is still considered part of the "west").

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Fri Dec 16, 2011 3:55 am The zx rip isn't the only one now, there's also "bloat" and "OZC". Anyhow, I think the dynit release looks pretty good. Alternative you can find a custom subbed R2J release. I find it interesting that the R2J DTS re-release is slightly less "cropped" on the sides.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Fri Dec 16, 2011 5:56 am Hi, I'll just say that the zx is really a release to get if you're going to that route. That being said, I'll also leave these two here: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11754/Finding-affordable-copy-of-EoE/0/?postdays=0&postorder=asc&highlight=[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/post/484586/Recent-Hauls-2/?highlight=#484586[/url]
